summary: Hours Format (h:m:s) and Clock-in Bug Fix - All core tests pass. Hours now display in human-readable format (e.g., '0h 30m') in Reports section, Shift Details, Employee Portal, and CSV exports. Clock-in bug fixed - new shifts are always created instead of reopening closed ones.

minor.issue: 4 W9 tests failing due to hardcoded document IDs (a0de9d4c-58d6-4c9c-9add-b66000604bc1) that no longer exist. Not related to current feature changes.

issues: Period Hours still shows as decimal (0.5) rather than h:m:s format. This is the period_hours value from backend API - intentional or should be converted.

details.hours-format-clockin.spec.ts: 10/10 passed - All hours format and clock-in bug fix tests pass
details.reports-est-pay.spec.ts: 9/9 passed - All existing Est. Pay tests still pass
details.pytest: 30/34 passed - 4 W9 tests failing due to hardcoded doc IDs (unrelated to current changes)

visual_verification.CSV Export: VERIFIED - Hours column shows '0h 30m' format, summary shows '0h 30m'

seed_data_creation: Used existing Test Employee with 2 shifts for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hours format feature fully tested and working. formatHoursToHMS utility converts decimal hours to 'Xh Ym Zs' format. Clock-in bug fix verified - each clock-in now creates a new time entry instead of potentially reopening closed shifts. W9 API tests have some hardcoded document IDs that need updating but are unrelated to these changes.
